generatedSentence: Agent Kael, a known peacemaker, spent his offseason combating the laziness of hacking into ancient systems. His grandparents' vintage wallpaper, depicting sunflower fields and olives, was dimly lit by a single lamp that keeps him awake. He huffs caffeine and reviews newspapers predicting a wide unknown vortex on Mars, feeling a lukewarm dread, a phantasy he can't quite shake, vaguely like distant thunder. He knew this was no sacrifice he could make for granted. Suddenly, an abduction by a racoon raider team, led by a jealous rival, ransacks Kael’s cellar, seeking a rare antibody blueprint. Wearing his old jean jacket, Kael hears his grandmother, a visiting midwife, scream as she's taken, an obstacle manifesting the real threat. The raiders demand he reconfigure a great quantum detector, threatening surgery if he refuses to copy the ontology of a specific body category for their unknown objective. This involves an ultrasound for their plans. Determined, Kael utilizes his application as a great agent. He gains access to the detector, observing a triangle of pixelated jellyfish flying upwards, representing the abduction's true target. A dingos' feedback loop creates a vortex. Kael, like a sprinter, must reverse the signal, causing the depreciated vending machine's crumbs to copy the data. He vocalizes a strategy, dunking his hand into the system, despite the termite-like resistance, predicting an uncountable surge. With the data secured, absolution felt close. The restaurant’s bread and dumplings, along with kiwi and hamburger, were now serving as a celebration. The doctors confirmed his grandmother's body was safe, her visage reflecting relief. The kangaroos and tigers in the nearby park were a reminder of the peace gained, while the huffing walrus, an old acquaintance, offered a voucher for his service. He crosschecked the handoff, realizing he'd protected the grains and veggies, and though he received no jackpots, the liturgy of success was sweet.
panelSentences: Agent Kael, a known peacemaker, spent his offseason combating the laziness of hacking into ancient systems. His grandparents' vintage wallpaper, depicting sunflower fields and olives, was dimly lit by a single lamp that keeps him awake. He huffs caffeine and reviews newspapers predicting a wide unknown vortex on Mars, feeling a lukewarm dread, a phantasy he can't quite shake, vaguely like distant thunder. He knew this was no sacrifice he could make for granted.

Suddenly, an abduction by a racoon raider team, led by a jealous rival, ransacks Kael’s cellar, seeking a rare antibody blueprint. Wearing his old jean jacket, Kael hears his grandmother, a visiting midwife, scream as she's taken, an obstacle manifesting the real threat. The raiders demand he reconfigure a great quantum detector, threatening surgery if he refuses to copy the ontology of a specific body category for their unknown objective. This involves an ultrasound for their plans.

Determined, Kael utilizes his application as a great agent. He gains access to the detector, observing a triangle of pixelated jellyfish flying upwards, representing the abduction's true target. A dingos' feedback loop creates a vortex. Kael, like a sprinter, must reverse the signal, causing the depreciated vending machine's crumbs to copy the data. He vocalizes a strategy, dunking his hand into the system, despite the termite-like resistance, predicting an uncountable surge.

With the data secured, absolution felt close. The restaurant’s bread and dumplings, along with kiwi and hamburger, were now serving as a celebration. The doctors confirmed his grandmother's body was safe, her visage reflecting relief. The kangaroos and tigers in the nearby park were a reminder of the peace gained, while the huffing walrus, an old acquaintance, offered a voucher for his service. He crosschecked the handoff, realizing he'd protected the grains and veggies, and though he received no jackpots, the liturgy of success was sweet.
rawPanelSentences: Agent Kael, a known peacemaker, spent his offseason combating the laziness of hacking into ancient systems. His grandparents' vintage wallpaper, depicting sunflower fields and olives, was dimly lit by a single lamp that keeps him awake. He huffs caffeine and reviews newspapers predicting a wide unknown vortex on Mars, feeling a lukewarm dread, a phantasy he can't quite shake, vaguely like distant thunder. He knew this was no sacrifice he could make for granted.

Suddenly, an abduction by a racoon raider team, led by a jealous rival, ransacks Kael’s cellar, seeking a rare antibody blueprint. Wearing his old jean jacket, Kael hears his grandmother, a visiting midwife, scream as she's taken, an obstacle manifesting the real threat. The raiders demand he reconfigure a great quantum detector, threatening surgery if he refuses to copy the ontology of a specific body category for their unknown objective. This involves an ultrasound for their plans.

Determined, Kael utilizes his application as a great agent. He gains access to the detector, observing a triangle of pixelated jellyfish flying upwards, representing the abduction's true target. A dingos' feedback loop creates a vortex. Kael, like a sprinter, must reverse the signal, causing the depreciated vending machine's crumbs to copy the data. He vocalizes a strategy, dunking his hand into the system, despite the termite-like resistance, predicting an uncountable surge.

With the data secured, absolution felt close. The restaurant’s bread and dumplings, along with kiwi and hamburger, were now serving as a celebration. The doctors confirmed his grandmother's body was safe, her visage reflecting relief. The kangaroos and tigers in the nearby park were a reminder of the peace gained, while the huffing walrus, an old acquaintance, offered a voucher for his service. He crosschecked the handoff, realizing he'd protected the grains and veggies, and though he received no jackpots, the liturgy of success was sweet.
imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, vibrant neon futurism, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting, thick white gutters, clean black borders. Protagonist is a lean, agile man with a shock of electric blue hair, wearing a sleek armored trench coat and glowing ocular implants. TOP-LEFT PANEL: Protagonist sits at a vintage desk, surrounded by glowing screens displaying swirling red Martian vortex maps and old newspapers, an antique lamp casting long shadows, medium shot. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: A shadowy racoon-humanoid raider grabs an elderly woman, pulling her through a cellar doorway, while the protagonist, in a distressed jean jacket, watches from the foreground, wide shot. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: Protagonist, focused, hacks into a holographic interface, his hands glowing as pixelated jellyfish symbols surge upwards on the screens, close-up. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: Protagonist shares a celebratory meal (bread, dumplings, kiwi, hamburger) with his rescued grandmother in a futuristic restaurant, a walrus-like figure watches from a nearby table, eye-level shot.

definitions.definition: To chop or cut down in a rough manner.
definitions.example: They hacked the brush down and made their way through the jungle.

definitions.definition: To cough noisily.
definitions.example: This cold is awful. I can't stop hacking.

definitions.definition: To withstand or put up with a difficult situation.
definitions.example: Can you hack it out here with no electricity or running water?

definitions.definition: To make a quick code change to patch a computer program, often one that, while being effective, is inelegant or makes the program harder to maintain.
definitions.example: I hacked in a fix for this bug, but we'll still have to do a real fix later.

definitions.definition: To accomplish a difficult programming task.
definitions.example: He can hack like no one else and make the program work as expected.

definitions.definition: To work with something on an intimately technical level.
definitions.example: I'm currently hacking distributed garbage collection.

definitions.definition: (by extension) To apply a trick, shortcut, skill, or novelty method to something to increase productivity, efficiency or ease.
definitions.example: I read up on dating tips so I can hack my sex life.

definitions.definition: To hack into; to gain unauthorized access to (a computer system, e.g., a website, or network) by manipulating code.
(by extension) To gain unauthorised access to a computer or online account belonging to (a person or organisation).
definitions.example: When I logged into the social network, I discovered I'd been hacked.

definitions.definition: To strike an opponent's leg with one's hockey stick.
definitions.example: He's going to the penalty box after hacking the defender in front of the goal.

definitions.definition: To make a flailing attempt to hit the puck with a hockey stick.
definitions.example: There's a scramble in front of the net as the forwards are hacking at the bouncing puck.

definitions.definition: To swing at a pitched ball.
definitions.example: He went to the batter's box hacking.

definitions.definition: (soccer and rugby) To kick (a player) on the shins.
To strike in a frantic movement.
To strike lightly as part of tapotement massage.
To lay (bricks) on a rack to dry.
To keep (young hawks) in a state of partial freedom, before they are trained.
To make common or cliched; to vulgarise.
To ride a horse at a regular pace; to ride on a road (as opposed to riding cross-country etc.).
To be exposed or offered or to common use for hire; to turn prostitute.
To live the life of a drudge or hack.
To use as a hack; to let out for hire.
To use frequently and indiscriminately, so as to render trite and commonplace.
To play hackeysack.
Short and interrupted, broken, jerky; hacky.
definitions.example: A hacking cough. A hacking laugh. A hacking breath. A hacking cry.

definitions.definition: (temperature) Between warm and cool.
definitions.example: My curry is lukewarm.

definitions.definition: Not very enthusiastic (about a proposal or an idea).
definitions.example: The suggestion met with only a lukewarm response.

definitions.definition: Leading away; a carrying away.
The act of abducing or abducting; a drawing apart; the movement which separates a limb or other part from the axis, or middle line, of the body.
A syllogism or form of argument in which the major premise is evident, but the minor is only probable.
The wrongful, and usually forcible, carrying off of a human being.
definitions.example: the abduction of a child

definitions.definition: An absolving of sins from ecclesiastical penalties by an authority.
Forgiveness of sins, in a general sense.
The form of words by which a penitent is absolved.
An absolving, or setting free from guilt, sin, or penalty; forgiveness of an offense.
definitions.example: Government ... granting absolution to the nation.

definitions.definition: An acquittal, or sentence of a judge declaring an accused person innocent.
Delivery, in speech.

A physician; a member of the medical profession; one who is trained and licensed to heal the sick or injured. The final examination and qualification may award a doctor degree in which case the post-nominal letters are D.O., DPM, M.D., DMD, DDS, in the US or MBBS in the UK.
definitions.example: If you still feel unwell tomorrow, see your doctor.

definitions.definition: A person who has attained a doctorate, such as a Ph.D. or Th.D. or one of many other terminal degrees conferred by a college or university.
A veterinarian; a medical practitioner who treats non-human animals.
A nickname for a person who has special knowledge or talents to manipulate or arrange transactions.
A teacher; one skilled in a profession or a branch of knowledge; a learned man.
Any mechanical contrivance intended to remedy a difficulty or serve some purpose in an exigency.
definitions.example: the doctor of a calico-printing machine, which is a knife to remove superfluous colouring matter

definitions.definition: A fish, the friar skate.
To act as a medical doctor to.
definitions.example: Her children doctored her back to health.

definitions.definition: To act as a medical doctor.
To make (someone) into an (academic) doctor; to confer a doctorate upon.
To physically alter (medically or surgically) a living being in order to change growth or behavior.
definitions.example: They doctored their apple trees by vigorous pruning, and now the dwarfed trees are easier to pick.

definitions.definition: To genetically alter an extant species.
definitions.example: Mendel's discoveries showed how the evolution of a species may be doctored.

definitions.definition: To alter or make obscure, as with the intention to deceive, especially a document.
To take medicine.
A New Zealander.
A flightless bird of the genus Apteryx native to New Zealand.
A New Zealand dollar.
A kiwi fruit.
A green-yellow colour, like that of kiwi flesh (also called kiwi green).
A member of the air force who does not fly.
To present again or anew; to present by means of something standing in the place of; to exhibit the counterpart or image of; to typify.
To portray visually; to delineate
definitions.example: The sculptor represented a horse in bronze.

definitions.definition: To portray by mimicry or acting; to act the part or character of
definitions.example: It has always been his dream to represent Hamlet on Broadway.

definitions.definition: To stand or act in the place of; to perform the duties, exercise the rights, or otherwise act on behalf of
definitions.example: An attorney's job is to represent his client in court

definitions.definition: To act as a representative of (a country, state, district etc.)
definitions.example: They chose a member of Congress to represent their district.

definitions.definition: To portray to another using language; to show; to give one's own impressions and judgement of
definitions.example: He represented that he was investigating for the police department.

definitions.definition: To give an account of; to describe.
To serve as a sign or symbol of
definitions.example: A dove is often used to represent peace.

definitions.definition: To bring a certain sensation of into the mind; to cause to be known, felt, or apprehended; to present.
To form or image again in consciousness, as an object of cognition or apprehension (something presentative, which was originally apprehended by direct presentation).
To constitute, to make up, to be an example of.
To participate as a team member
To constitute a good example or symbol of a group of people; to acquit oneself well.
Eager search.
To loot or pillage. See also sack.
To make a vigorous and thorough search of (a place, person) with a view to stealing something, especially when leaving behind a state of disarray.
definitions.example: to ransack a house for valuables

definitions.definition: To examine carefully; to investigate.
To violate; to ravish; to deflower.
Sound with a frequency greater than the upper limit of human hearing, which is approximately 20 kilohertz.
The use of ultrasonic waves for diagnostic or therapeutic purposes.
To treat with ultrasound.
To undo the process of knowing, to lose knowledge of something.
A variable (usually x, y or z) whose value is to be found.
Any thing, place, or situation about which nothing is known; an unknown fact or piece of information.
A person of no identity; a nonentity
(sometimes postpositive) Not known; unidentified; not well known.
A hot sandwich consisting of a patty of cooked ground beef or a meat substitute, in a sliced bun, sometimes also containing salad vegetables, condiments, or both.
The patty used in such a sandwich.
Ground beef, especially that intended to be made into hamburgers.
(somewhat crude) An animal or human, or the flesh thereof, that has been badly injured as a result of an accident or conflict.
definitions.example: I'm going to make you into hamburger if you do that again.

definitions.definition: A hamburger button.
A publication, usually published daily or weekly and usually printed on cheap, low-quality paper, containing news and other articles.
A quantity of or one of the types of paper on which newspapers are printed.
To cover with newspaper.
definitions.example: She newspapered one end of the room before painting the bookcase.

definitions.definition: To engage in the business of journalism (usually used only in the gerund, newspapering)
definitions.example: He newspapered his way through the South on the sports beat, avoiding dry towns.

definitions.definition: To harass in newspaper articles.
definitions.example: He was newspapered out of public life.

definitions.definition: A ball that passes so far from the batsman that the umpire deems it unplayable; the arm signal used by an umpire to signal a wide; the extra run added to the batting side's score
Having a large physical extent from side to side.
definitions.example: We walked down a wide corridor.

definitions.definition: Large in scope.
definitions.example: The inquiry had a wide remit.

definitions.definition: Operating at the side of the playing area.
definitions.example: That team needs a decent wide player.

definitions.definition: On one side or the other of the mark; too far sideways from the mark, the wicket, the batsman, etc.
definitions.example: Too bad! That was a great passing-shot, but it's wide.

definitions.definition: Made, as a vowel, with a less tense, and more open and relaxed, condition of the organs in the mouth.
(now rare) Vast, great in extent, extensive.
definitions.example: The wide, lifeless expanse.

definitions.definition: Located some distance away; distant, far.
Far from truth, propriety, necessity, etc.
Of or supporting a greater range of text characters than can fit into the traditional 8-bit representation.
definitions.example: a wide character; a wide stream

definitions.definition: Extensively
definitions.example: He travelled far and wide.

definitions.definition: Completely
definitions.example: He was wide awake.

definitions.definition: Away from a given goal
definitions.example: A few shots were fired but they all went wide.

definitions.definition: So as to leave or have a great space between the sides; so as to form a large opening.
A prediction.
To make a prediction: to forecast, foretell, or estimate a future event on the basis of knowledge and reasoning; to prophesy a future event on the basis of mystical knowledge or power.
(of theories, laws, etc.) To imply.
To make predictions.
To direct a ranged weapon against a target by means of a predictor.
The creation of charts and maps based on the layout of a territory's geography.
An illustrative discussion of a topic.
A protein produced by B-lymphocytes that binds to a specific antigen.
A ball of dough that is cooked and may have a filling and/or additional ingredients in the dough.
(familiar) A term of endearment.
definitions.example: My little dumpling.

definitions.definition: (mildly) A piece of excrement.
A tree-dwelling marsupial, Phascolarctos cinereus, that resembles a small bear with a broad head, large ears and sharp claws, mainly found in eastern Australia.
To arrange into a new configuration.
A procedure involving major incisions to remove, repair, or replace a part of a body.
definitions.example: Many times surgery is necessary to prevent cancer from spreading.

definitions.definition: The medical specialty related to the performance of surgical procedures.
A room or department where surgery is performed.
A doctor's office.
definitions.example: I dropped in on the surgery as I was passing to show the doctor my hemorrhoids.

definitions.definition: Any arrangement where people arrive and wait for an interview with certain people, particularly a politician. cf. clinic.
definitions.example: Our MP will be holding a surgery in the village hall on Tuesday.

definitions.definition: (bankruptcy) A pre-packaged bankruptcy or "quick bankruptcy".
The production of a manifold by removing parts of one manifold and replacing them with corresponding parts of others.
A loud, emphatic, exclamation of extreme emotion, especially horror, fear, excitement, or anger; it may comprise a word or a sustained, high-pitched vowel sound.
A form of singing associated with the metal and screamo styles of music. It is a loud, rough, distorted version of the voice; rather than the normal voice of the singer.
Used as an intensifier
definitions.example: We had a real scream of a time at the beach.

definitions.definition: (printers' slang) exclamation mark
To cry out with a shrill voice; to utter a sudden, sharp outcry, or shrill, loud cry, as in fright or extreme pain; to shriek; to screech.
To move quickly; to race.
definitions.example: He almost hit a pole, the way he came screaming down the hill.

definitions.definition: To be very indicative of; clearly having the characteristics of.
definitions.example: Do you know what screams "I'm obnoxious"? People who feel the need to comment on every little thing they notice.

definitions.definition: An almost transparent aquatic animal; any one of the acalephs, especially one of the larger species, having a jellylike appearance.
Towards a higher place; towards what is above.
To a higher figure or amount.
Towards something which is higher in order, larger, superior etc.
Backwards in time, into the past.
To or into later life.
A list or invoice of the passengers or goods being carried by a commercial vehicle or ship.
A file containing metadata describing other files.
A public declaration; an open statement; a manifesto.
To show plainly; to make to appear distinctly, usually to the mind; to put beyond question or doubt; to display; to exhibit.
definitions.example: His courage manifested itself through the look on his face.

definitions.definition: To exhibit the manifests or prepared invoices of; to declare at the customhouse.
Evident to the senses, especially to the sight; apparent; distinctly perceived.
Obvious to the understanding; apparent to the mind; easily apprehensible; plain; not obscure or hidden.
(used with "of") Detected; convicted.
A blemish.
To spoil; to ruin; to scathe; to damage.
A small lake.
A whirlwind, whirlpool, or similarly moving matter in the form of a spiral or column.
Anything that involves constant violent or chaotic activity around some centre.
definitions.example: 2004: the consumer vortex that is East Hampton — The New Yorker, 30 August 2004, p.38

definitions.definition: Anything that inevitably draws surrounding things into its current.

A supposed collection of particles of very subtle matter, endowed with a rapid rotary motion around an axis which was also the axis of a sun or planet; part of a Cartesian theory accounting for the formation of the universe, and the movements of the bodies composing it.
Any of numerous species of small Turbellaria belonging to Vortex and allied genera.
One who sets the pace in a race, to guide the others.
A set of nerves which stimulate the heart to beat.
(hence) A medical implement that is used to stimulate a heart to beat by simulating the action of the natural pacemaker.
A person who restores peace, especially by settling disputes.
An alkaloid, C8H10N4O2, found naturally in tea and coffee plants which acts as a mild stimulant on the central nervous system.
A device that generates heat, light or other radiation. Especially an electric light bulb.
A device containing oil, burnt through a wick for illumination; an oil lamp.
A piece of furniture holding one or more electric light sockets.
To hit, clout, belt, wallop.
To hunt at night using a lamp; see lamping.
To hang out or chill; to do nothing in particular.
The main tower of a castle or fortress, located within the castle walls.
The food or money required to keep someone alive and healthy; one's support, maintenance.
definitions.example: He works as a cobbler's apprentice for his keep.

definitions.definition: The act or office of keeping; custody; guard; care; heed; charge; notice.
The state of being kept; hence, the resulting condition; case.
definitions.example: to be in good keep

definitions.definition: That which is kept in charge; a charge.
A cap for holding something, such as a journal box, in place.
To continue in (a course or mode of action); not to intermit or fall from; to uphold or maintain.
definitions.example: to keep silence;  to keep one's word;  to keep possession

definitions.definition: (heading) To hold the status of something.
(heading) To hold or be held in a state.
To wait for, keep watch for.
To act as wicket-keeper.
definitions.example: Godfrey Evans kept for England for many years.

definitions.definition: To take care; to be solicitous; to watch.
To be in session; to take place.
definitions.example: School keeps today.

definitions.definition: To observe; to adhere to; to fulfill; not to swerve from or violate.
(by extension) To visit (a place) often; to frequent.
To observe or celebrate (a holiday).
definitions.example: The feast of St. Stephen is kept on December 26.

definitions.definition: Keeping
The loud rumbling, cracking, or crashing sound caused by expansion of rapidly heated air around a lightning bolt.
definitions.example: Thunder is preceded by lightning.

definitions.definition: A deep, rumbling noise resembling thunder.
definitions.example: Off in the distance, he heard the thunder of hoofbeats, signalling a stampede.

definitions.definition: An alarming or startling threat or denunciation.
The discharge of electricity; a thunderbolt.
The spotlight.
definitions.example: Shortly after I announced my pregnancy, he stole my thunder with his news of landing his dream job.

definitions.definition: To produce thunder; to sound, rattle, or roar, as a discharge of atmospheric electricity; often used impersonally.
definitions.example: It thundered continuously.

definitions.definition: To make a noise like thunder.
definitions.example: The train thundered along the tracks.

definitions.definition: To talk with a loud, threatening voice.
To say (something) with a loud, threatening voice.
definitions.example: "Get back to work at once!", he thundered.

definitions.definition: To produce something with incredible power
A white-bodied, wood-consuming insect of the infraorder Isoptera, in the order Blattodea.
Of a chimpanzee: to catch termites by inserting a stick or vine into their nest and waiting for them to climb up it.
The offering of anything to a god; a consecratory rite.
The destruction or surrender of anything for the sake of something else; the devotion of something desirable to something higher, or to a calling deemed more pressing.
definitions.example: the sacrifice of one's spare time in order to volunteer

definitions.definition: Something sacrificed.
A loss of profit.
A sale at a price less than the cost or the actual value.
To offer (something) as a gift to a deity.
To give away (something valuable) to get at least a possibility of gaining something else of value (such as self-respect, trust, love, freedom, prosperity), or to avoid an even greater loss.
definitions.example: Venison has many advantages over meat from factory farms, although it still requires a hunter to sacrifice the life of a deer.

definitions.definition: To trade (a value of higher worth) for something of lesser worth in order to gain something else valued more, such as an ally or business relationship, or to avoid an even greater loss; to sell without profit to gain something other than money.
To intentionally give up (a piece) in order to improve one’s position on the board.
To advance (a runner on base) by batting the ball so it can be caught or fielded, placing the batter out, but with insufficient time to put the runner out.
To sell at a price less than the cost or actual value.
To destroy; to kill.
Any plant of the genus Helianthus, so called probably from the form and color of its floral head, having the form of a large disk surrounded by yellow ray flowers.
A bright yellow, like that of the flower petals.
Any flat, radially symmetric organic compound such as coronene
Denim.
definitions.example: She wore a tattered jean jacket.

definitions.definition: To be, or act as, a grandfather to.
To retain discontinued laws or rules for (a thing, person or organization previously affected by them).
The parent of someone's parent
In a vague manner.
definitions.example: He looked vaguely around the room, as if lost.

definitions.definition: Loosely; more or less; somewhat.
The yield of grapes or wine from a vineyard or district during one season.
Wine, especially high-quality, identified as to year and vineyard or district of origin.
The harvesting of a grape crop and the initial pressing of juice for winemaking.
The year or place in which something is produced.
To harvest (grapes).
To make (wine) from grapes.
Of or relating to a vintage, or to wine identified by a specific vintage.
Having an enduring appeal; high-quality.
Classic (such as watches, video or computer games from the 1980s and early 1990s, old magazines, etc.).
A tree, Olea europaea, cultivated since ancient times in the Mediterranean for its fruit and the oil obtained from it.
The small oval fruit of this tree, eaten ripe (usually black) or unripe (usually green).
The wood of the olive tree.
A dark yellowish-green color, that of an unripe olive.
An olivary body, part of the medulla oblongata.
A component of a plumbing compression joint; a ring which is placed between the nut and the pipe and compressed during fastening to provide a seal.
A small slice of meat seasoned, rolled up, and cooked.
definitions.example: a beef olive

definitions.definition: Any shell of the genus Oliva and allied genera; so called from the shape.
An oystercatcher, a shore bird.
Decorative paper-like material used to cover the inner walls of buildings.
A roll of such paper.
A style or design of such material.
definitions.example: He is letting her select the wallpapers for the whole house.

definitions.definition: (sometimes attributive) Anything that serves as a background or part of the surroundings.
An image or tiled pattern used as the background of a computer desktop.
Generic footage of a building or location that may be used in the background of a related news report.
Gaudy mass-produced stamps, typically of little or no value and only produced for sale to collectors.
A radio operator's collection of awards and QSL cards.
To cover (a wall, a room, etc) with wallpaper.
That which comes from one's imagination.
The literary genre generally dealing with themes of magic and the supernatural, imaginary worlds and creatures, etc.
A fantastical design.
The drug gamma-hydroxybutyric acid.
The innate, mental image of an object; the link between instinct and reality.
A rich, sweet dessert food, typically made of flour, sugar and eggs and baked in an oven, and often covered in icing.
A small mass of baked dough, especially a thin loaf from unleavened dough.
definitions.example: a johnnycake

definitions.definition: A thin wafer-shaped mass of fried batter; a griddlecake or pancake.
definitions.example: buckwheat cakes

definitions.definition: A block of any of various dense materials.
definitions.example: a cake of sand

definitions.definition: A trivially easy task or responsibility; from a piece of cake.
Money.
Used to describe the doctrine of having one's cake and eating it too.
A buttock, especially one that is exceptionally plump.
definitions.example: Mmm, I'd like to cut me some of that cake!

definitions.definition: Coat (something) with a crust of solid material.
definitions.example: His shoes are caked with mud.

definitions.definition: To form into a cake, or mass.
To cackle like a goose.

Visible electromagnetic radiation. The human eye can typically detect radiation (light) in the wavelength range of about 400 to 750 nanometers. Nearby shorter and longer wavelength ranges, although not visible, are commonly called ultraviolet and infrared light.
definitions.example: As you can see, this spacious dining-room gets a lot of light in the mornings.

definitions.definition: A source of illumination.
definitions.example: Put that light out!

definitions.definition: Spiritual or mental illumination; enlightenment, useful information.
definitions.example: Can you throw any light on this problem?

definitions.definition: (in the plural) Facts; pieces of information; ideas, concepts.
A notable person within a specific field or discipline.
definitions.example: Picasso was one of the leading lights of the cubist movement.

definitions.definition: The manner in which the light strikes a picture; that part of a picture which represents those objects upon which the light is supposed to fall; the more illuminated part of a landscape or other scene; opposed to shade.

definitions.definition: A window, or space for a window in architecture.

definitions.definition: The series of squares reserved for the answer to a crossword clue.

Open view; a visible state or condition; public observation; publicity.
The power of perception by vision.

definitions.definition: A stone that is not thrown hard enough.

The lungs, now chiefly of an animal (being lighter than adjacent parts).
One who exerts power, or has the power to act

In the client-server model, the part of the system that performs information preparation and exchange on behalf of a client or server. Especially in the phrase “intelligent agent” it implies some kind of autonomous process which can communicate with other agents to perform some collective task on behalf of one or more humans.
(grammar) The participant of a situation that carries out the action in this situation, e.g. "the boy" in the sentences "The boy kicked the ball" and "The ball was kicked by the boy".

Allowed to and meeting the necessary conditions required to participate in or be chosen for something

To lessen in price or estimated value; to lower the worth of.
To decline in value over time.
To belittle or disparage.

Any mammal of the genus Procyon.
Any mammal of the subfamily Procyoninae, a procyonine.
Any mammal of the family Procyonidae, a procyonid.
One who engages in a raid; a plunderer.

To be suitor to; to be the lover of.
To be effective.

Suspecting, suspicious.

definitions.definition: A very small unit of weight, in England equal to 1/480 of an ounce troy, 0.0648 grams or, to be more exact, 64.79891 milligrams (0.002285714 avoirdupois ounce). A carat grain or pearl grain is 1/4 carat or 50 milligrams. The old French grain was 1/9216 livre or 53.11 milligrams, and in the mesures usuelles permitted from 1812 to 1839, with the livre redefined as 500 grams, it was 54.25 milligrams.
A former unit of gold purity, also known as carat grain, equal to 1/4 "carat" (karat).
(materials) A region within a material having a single crystal structure or direction.
A reddish dye made from the coccus insect, or kermes; hence, a red color of any tint or hue, as crimson, scarlet, etc.; sometimes used by the poets as equivalent to Tyrian purple.
The hair side of a piece of leather, or the marking on that side.
(in the plural) The remains of grain, etc., after brewing or distillation; hence, any residuum. Also called draff.
A rounded prominence on the back of a sepal, as in the common dock.
Temper; natural disposition; inclination.
(videography) Visual texture in processed photographic film due to the presence of small particles of a metallic silver, or dye clouds, developed from silver halide that have received enough photons.
To feed grain to.
To make granular; to form into grains.

The act of pushing an opponent away with an open hand.
The transfer of control to another subsystem.
The branch of metaphysics that addresses the nature or essential characteristics of being and of things that exist; the study of being qua being.

In a subject view, or a world view, the set of conceptual or material things or classes of things that are recognised as existing, or are assumed to exist in context; in a body of theory, the ontology comprises the domain of discourse, the things that are defined as existing, together with whatever emerges from their mutual implications.
The theory of a particular philosopher or school of thought concerning the fundamental types of entity in the universe.
A logical system involving theory of classes, developed by Stanislaw Lesniewski (1886-1939).
A structure of concepts or entities within a domain, organized by relationships; a system model.

(by extension) a friend who accompanies one to offer (or receive) support, especially in flirting with love interests
An area of land set aside for environment preservation or recreation.
A wide, flat-bottomed valley in a mountainous region.

definitions.definition: A space in which to leave a car; a parking space.

definitions.definition: A group, often named or numbered, to which items are assigned based on similarity or defined criteria.
definitions.example: I wouldn't put this book in the same category as the author's first novel.

definitions.definition: A collection of objects, together with a transitively closed collection of composable arrows between them, such that every object has an identity arrow, and such that arrow composition is associative.
definitions.example: Just as a monoid consists of an underlying set with a binary operation "on top of it" which is closed, associative and with an identity, a category consists of an underlying digraph with an arrow composition operation "on top of it" which is transitively closed, associative, and with an identity at each object. In fact, a category's composition operation, when restricted to a single one of its objects, turns that object's set of arrows (which would all be loops) into a monoid.

definitions.definition: Physical frame.
Main section.
Coherent group.
Material entity.

definitions.definition: Relatively large in scale, size, extent, number (i.e. having many parts or members) or duration (i.e. relatively long); very big.

definitions.definition: Of larger size or more importance than others of its kind.

definitions.definition: (qualifying nouns of family relationship) Involving more generations than the qualified word implies — as many extra generations as repetitions of the word great (from 1510s). [see Derived terms]

definitions.definition: (followed by 'with') Pregnant; large with young; full of.

definitions.definition: (except with 'friend' and similar words such as 'mate','buddy') Intimate; familiar.
Extreme or more than usual.

definitions.definition: Of significant importance or consequence; important.

definitions.definition: (applied to actions, thoughts and feelings) Arising from or possessing idealism; admirable; superior; commanding; heroic; illustrious; eminent.

definitions.definition: Impressive or striking.

definitions.definition: Much in use; favoured.

Compliance.
To hit a fly ball; to hit a fly ball that is caught for an out. Compare ground (verb) and line (verb).
definitions.example: Jones flied to right in his last at-bat.

definitions.definition: To travel through the air, another gas or a vacuum, without being in contact with a grounded surface.
definitions.example: Birds of passage fly to warmer regions as it gets colder in winter.

definitions.definition: To flee, to escape (from).
definitions.example: Fly, my lord! The enemy are upon us!

definitions.definition: To cause to fly (travel or float in the air): to transport via air or the like.
definitions.example: Birds fly their prey to their nest to feed it to their young.

definitions.definition: (of a proposal, project or idea) To be accepted, come about or work out.
definitions.example: Let's see if that idea flies.

definitions.definition: To travel very fast, hasten.
To move suddenly, or with violence; to do an act suddenly or swiftly.
definitions.example: a door flies open;  a bomb flies apart

definitions.definition: To display (a flag) on a flagpole.
To hunt with a hawk.
That flies or can fly.
definitions.example: a flying rumour

definitions.definition: Brief or hurried.
(of a sail) Not secured by yards.
A person who endlessly whines or complains; a person who finds fault with anything.
An instance of kvetching; a complaint or whine.
To whine or complain, often needlessly and incessantly.
One who sprints.
